text.tspan: Local-first Next.js monolith for trend-led and media-led video creation, storyboard planning, FFmpeg rendering, metadata
App Router UI + Route Handlers + Prisma +
local media files + Gemini/Veo + YouTube APIs.
Creator-facing pages, shared panels, and deployment note
Next.js Route Handlers running in the Node.js runtime
Workflow orchestration across content, media planning, rendering, and publishing
Data model, local assets, in-process jobs, and external systems
/onboarding for niche, tone, timezone, curated or custom RSS, and
/dashboard for trend-led and media-led creation, asset upload,
storyboarding, render control, metadata, and publishing.
/jobs/[id] for job history, logs, status, and render artifacts.
Client page code polls jobs and streams
Trend clustering, idea generation, storyboard coverage
Background render and upload jobs, metadata generation,
user.ts resolves or creates the local
rss.ts fetches RSS or Atom entries.
gaps with stills or Veo motion clips.
ffmpeg.ts probes source media and invokes
Logs and outputJson are written back
RSS or Atom feeds provide trend inputs through rss-parser.
Gemini chat, image, TTS, and Veo video endpoints power idea
generation, metadata, narration, and support media.
Google OAuth 2.0 and YouTube Data API v3 handle account
Local ffmpeg and ffprobe binaries handle sampling,
composition, subtitle burn-in, mixing, and final render
to YouTube or stay in mock
Generated from the checked-in project structure and runtime code paths.
